interactions.summary: Jiang contrasts Greek symposium and Roman feast, then answers questions about great men, Alexander as Achilles, Scandinavian cohesion, and whether AI changes imperial decline.
YouTube/class questions ask about great men, Alexander’s relation to the Iliad, Scandinavian difference, and AI/digital empires.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Student or YouTube question as voiced in class

claims.claim: Athenian fun is conversation, philosophy, and watered wine; Roman fun is vomiting, orgy, and mythic reenactment amid public starvation.
Jiang ranks poet-prophets and civilization-makers such as Homer, Dante, Plato, Jesus, Zarathustra, and Yahweh above conquerors like Caesar, Alexander, and Napoleon.
Alexander acts out Achilles because he and others see him as the character who must seek glory by dying in battle.
Scandinavian cohesion comes from small scale, cold indoor sociability, cooperation under harsh climate, and surrounding enemies.
AI may increase imperial control in theory, but a dying empire lacks the energy to build real systems and instead produces scams or psychological matrices.

signature_moments.moment: Roman feast is read as social reenactment of founding violation.
signature_moments.source_phrase: reenactment of the rape of Sabine woman
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Links entertainment, myth, and elite cohesion.

signature_moments.moment: Greatness belongs to those who create civilizations through ideas, not merely conquerors.
signature_moments.source_phrase: they create civilizations based on their ideas
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Clarifies Jiang’s hierarchy of prophet-poet over military hero.

signature_moments.moment: A dying empire wants AI control but has energy only for scams.
signature_moments.source_phrase: a lot of desire and motivation to create an AI scam
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Carries a time-sensitive modern empire claim.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang’s highest category of historical greatness: figures whose ideas create civilizations.
Later-course topic named as psychological or technological enslavement through digital systems.

chronology_notes.note: Modern AI/digital technology is flagged as a topic Jiang says he will discuss toward the end of the course.
